0:
{
  "report": "Rule Name: Cross-site scripting
Rule Description: Writing user input directly to a web page allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.
Severity: error
Detailed Message: Cross-site scripting vulnerability due to a [["user-provided value"|"relative:///xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-repository/xwiki-platform-repository-server-api/src/main/java/org/xwiki/repository/internal/resources/ExtensionVersionFileRESTResource.java:170:29:170:47"]].
Location-file: /xwiki-platform-core/xwiki-platform-repository/xwiki-platform-repository-server-api/src/main/java/org/xwiki/repository/internal/resources/ExtensionVersionFileRESTResource.java
Location-start-line: 107
Location-start-col: 16
Location-end-line: 107
Location-end-col: 31",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The taint flow identified by CodeQL is real, but the reported CWE is not. `extensionId` comes from `@PathParam`, reaches `downloadLocalExtension()`, and is concatenated into the `Content-Disposition` response header before `response.build()`. However, this REST resource is a file-download endpoint, not a web-page renderer: it returns a streamed entity plus `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=...`, and it does not place `extensionId` into HTML, JavaScript, or any browser-executed page context. So this is not a real CWE-079/XSS condition. The false positive is not due to unreachable control flow; the key issue is that the sink is semantically misclassified as XSS.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "Source and path: `downloadExtension(@PathParam ... String extensionId, @PathParam ... String extensionVersion)` at lines 88-92 passes the values to `downloadLocalExtension(extensionId, extensionVersion)` at line 104. In the `ResourceType.URL` branch, the code builds a download response with `response.entity(entity.getContent())` at line 170 and `response.header(\"Content-Disposition\", \"attachment; filename=\\\"\" + extensionId + '-' + extensionVersion + '.' + extensionType + \"\\\"\")` at lines 171-172, then returns `response.build()` at line 107. This is an HTTP header on a download response, not HTML output. Additional context: the helper `getAttachmentResponse()` also uses the same `content-disposition` download-header pattern (AbstractExtensionRESTResource lines 790-798). Real usage: integration tests call `getUtil().rest().getBuffer(Resources.EXTENSION_VERSION_FILE, null, ..., ...)` and only assert the downloaded buffer length (RepositoryIT lines 253-254, 279-280, 311-312), confirming this endpoint is consumed as binary file content rather than rendered as a page. Reachability note: `RepositoryManager.getExistingExtensionDocumentById()` performs an exact lookup on the extension id and returns 404 if it does not exist (lines 256-283), so the endpoint is not blindly echoing arbitrary request text into a page.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}"
}
